Complete Go-to-Market Plan · June 2026 – 2027

Afro Baobab Cultural Hub

Everything in its right order. Ten things before September. The rest builds on what those ten create.

Target Opening: September 2026 Al Quoz, Dubai Objective: Awareness + Lead Gen Phased · Prioritised · Realistic
Tier 1
Do Now
June – August 2026
Tier 2
After Opening
October – December 2026
Tier 3
Future Growth
2027 and Beyond
Tier 1 · Do Now · June–August

The filter for every action right now is one question: does this directly generate a school booking, a corporate conversation, or a ceremony guest before September? If yes — do it now. If no — it waits. Ten things. Nothing else competes with these.

01
Today — overdue
School outreach — warm calls
Saran calls and WhatsApps every existing school contact. GEMS, Nord Anglia, Kent College, Blossom, Little Sprouts. Message: "We've built a permanent home — I want to reserve your dates before summer." Attach PDF + testimonials. Target: 20 pencilled dates before summer break.
02
Today — overdue
Honorary guest outreach
6–8 weeks minimum lead time. Three candidates in parallel — not sequentially. Guinean Ambassador is the warmest option. UAE Ministry of Culture as second. No ceremony without this confirmed.
03
This week
CRM setup
Zoho CRM. Two hours. Five segments: Schools, Corporates, Public, Artists, Media. Every contact from outreach flows in same day. Must be live before any further outreach — untracked leads are lost revenue.
04
Next week
Website ships to live domain
Stop iterating — ship what exists. Email capture, schools PDF, contact form. A live page beats a perfect unpublished one. Every school and corporate conversation needs a URL to point to.
05
This week
Post existing event photos today
The track record is the content. Drumming sessions, workshops, children's faces, diplomatic events — all of this proves the experience before the hub opens. No new assets needed. Post now.
06
This week
Google Business Profile live
New hub profile: Al Quoz location pinned, renders uploaded, "Opening Soon" status, website link. Takes two hours. Google needs 3 months to build trust — a profile created in September ranks nowhere. Also: WhatsApp past event clients for Google reviews this week.
07
July
African artists confirmed for ceremony
3–5 performers: drumming ensemble, kora player, spoken word poet. 5–8 exhibition artists for gallery zones. Artists are a marketing channel — every artist shares with their audience. Start outreach in July via Instagram DM and Alserkal community events.
08
July
Alserkal Avenue introduction
Walk into the management office — you're neighbours. Introduce the hub, express genuine respect for their community. Ask nothing yet. This seeds the most important local ecosystem relationship and opens school referral conversations for post-opening.
09
August 1
Press kit + one exclusive pitch
Brand story, renders, founder bio, fact sheet. Send to one journalist for an exclusive pre-opening story — Time Out Dubai or Brownbook. One well-placed story is worth more than ten generic mentions. Personal relationship approach, not a press release blast.
10
August (3 weeks before)
Ceremony invitations designed and sent
On-brand digital invitation to all 80–120 guests. VIPs get a personal call or WhatsApp from Saran alongside the invite. RSVP tracked centrally. Honorary guest was invited 6 weeks earlier — this is the general send.
Your Biggest Unfair Advantage

Not launching from zero

Most new cultural venues spend 3 years building what Afro Baobab already has. The hub is a graduation, not a start. Every outreach call is a reunion, not a cold pitch.

Proven Track Record · Dubai
Five years of delivery. Every contact is warm. Every lead is a reorder.
The outreach to schools isn't cold outreach — it's a reunion call. You're not selling something new to strangers; you're telling loyal clients you've built them a permanent home.
5+
Years of school programme delivery in Dubai
10+
Schools worked with — GEMS, Nord Anglia, Kent College
3
Published testimonials from educators and CEOs
1
Guinean Ambassador — warm relationship confirmed
"From the very first session it was clear this was something truly special. The impact on their confidence and oracy skills has been wonderful to witness."

School Educator · Dubai
"Afro Baobab exceeded all my expectations! Their ability to craft immersive, culturally rich experiences is truly remarkable. Every detail executed with precision and passion."

Dubai

Lead Gen Foundation

What you promise before the hub opens

You're not selling a venue. You're selling a proven experience with a permanent home. The message is different for each audience — and it's already credible because of the track record.

To Schools

Reserve your dates before we open

"We are opening a permanent African cultural hub in Al Quoz in September. Based on our years working together, I want to reserve your school's dates for Term 1 before the summer — spaces are limited and schools are booking now."
No site visit needed. They're buying Saran's track record, not a building.
To Corporates

Book an experience or bring your team to learn

"We're opening a permanent African cultural hub in Al Quoz in September — a space for team experiences, cultural learning, and meaningful connection. I'd love to find the right programme for your team."
Lead with the experience. Quality proven through testimonials and track record.
To African Artists

Your work in Dubai's first African cultural hub

"We are opening Dubai's first permanent African cultural hub in September. We're inviting a select group of African artists to exhibit, perform, and collaborate — and to be present at the opening ceremony."
Artists amplify your reach. Their audiences become your audiences. Engage them early.
Infrastructure — This Week

CRM

Every school conversation, every WhatsApp reply is evaporating with no follow-up system. Set up before any further outreach — two hours, five segments, done.

Five segments. One pipeline.

Every contact from outreach, social, website, and events flows into one trackable system from today forward.

🏫 SchoolsIntro → curriculum doc → booking
🏢 CorporatesIntro → programme offer → proposal
👨‍👩‍👧 PublicWelcome → preview → booking
🎨 ArtistsInterest → call → partnership brief
📰 MediaPress kit → pitch → follow-up

How Every Lead Flows

01
Contact captured

WhatsApp, website form, event, DM — into CRM same day

02
Segment tagged

Each gets its own automated follow-up sequence

03
First email within 24 hrs

Brand-voiced, relevant PDF attached

04
Personal follow-up

Schools and corporates: call or WhatsApp within 48 hours

05
Status tracked

Contacted → Replied → Meeting → Pencilled → Confirmed

Production Plan

Marketing materials

Only what's needed, when it's needed. No printed brochures, no merchandise, no video series before opening. Every asset below has a specific job to do in the outreach and conversion pipeline.

Available Now — Use Today
✓ Ready
📄

Schools PDF & Learning Pack

Attach to every school outreach message today. This is the primary conversion tool for warm school calls — it shows curriculum alignment, programme descriptions, and past delivery evidence.

Use: every school WhatsApp and email, now
✓ Ready
🖼️

Space Renders (6 zones)

The renders of the storytelling lounge, drumming room, mask lab, gallery, café and jewellery space. Use on the website, in outreach messages, and in the press kit. Real photography replaces them in August.

Use: website, outreach, CRM emails, ceremony invitation
✓ Ready
📸

Existing Event Photography

5 years of real event photos — drumming sessions, workshops, children's faces, diplomatic events, community gatherings. Proves the experience before the hub opens. Post on social today.

Use: social media now, website, CRM emails
July — Blocking Live Outreach
🔴 Blocking
💼

School Pricing Sheet

One page. Programme tiers, group sizes, pricing, what's included. Every school conversation that doesn't have a price attached stalls. This must exist before any follow-up call happens.

Owner: agree pricing internally this week · Design: Meriem
Needed by: end of June / first week July
🔴 Blocking
🏢

Corporate One-Pager

One page. Three programme tiers — team experience, cultural workshop, private event. What happens, how long, how many people, indicative pricing. Without this corporate conversations don't convert.

Owner: content this week · Design: Meriem · July
Needed by: mid-July
July
✉️

Ceremony Invitation

Digital, on-brand. Cormorant Garamond headline, ivory and terracotta palette, clear date, time, RSVP link. VIP version for embassies and honorary guest. Sent 3 weeks before ceremony.

Design: Meriem · Brief: July · Send: 3 weeks before ceremony
Needed by: August 15
July
🎁

Guest Take-Home Gift

A small cultural object, handcrafted piece, or founding story card. Something guests keep and talk about. Source from African artists or craft suppliers. ~120 units needed.

Owner: Saran selects · Source: July · Ready: August
Needed by: 1 week before ceremony
August — Unlocks PR & Paid Ads
August 1
📰

Press Kit

One PDF. Contains: brand story (2 pages), high-res renders + photography when available, founder bio, fact sheet, quote bank, key statistics from events track record. Sent to all media contacts August 1.

Content: Nizar · Design: Meriem · Deadline: August 1
Needed by: August 1 — gates all PR outreach
🔴 Critical Asset
📷

Real Space Photography

The single most important asset in the entire plan. Professional photographer, full day shoot at 70% fit-out completion. Shot list: all 6 zones, entrance, café, exterior, and detail shots. Do not wait for full completion.

Book photographer now · Shoot: as soon as 70% complete · Edit: 1 week turnaround
Unlocks: website final polish, press kit, paid ads, ceremony invites
August
🎬

Brand Video — 60–90 Seconds

Cinematic, emotional, sensory. Music: live kora or original African-inspired score. No voiceover — let the space and people speak. Used for website hero, Instagram Reel, and press pitches. Shoot same day as photography.

Videographer: book with photographer · Shoot: same day · Edit: 1 week
Use: website hero, Instagram, press kit, ceremony content
August
🌐

Website — Full Brand Site

Home, Schools, Corporates, About, Experiences, Gallery, Contact. All pages live with real photography. Connected to Zoho CRM with active lead capture forms. No booking engine yet — just inquiry forms.

Gates: paid ads, PR, all outreach with a URL · Deadline: before mid-August ads
Live by: mid-August minimum
September — Ceremony Content
Ceremony Night
🎥

Ceremony Photography & Video

Dedicated photographer + videographer, full evening, shot list covering all 4 content moments. This is not the same as the brand shoot — this is live event documentation. Edited and delivered within 48 hours of the ceremony.

Brief: shot list for Threshold Moment, drumming, spoken word, ribbon-cutting · Delivery: 48 hours
Produces: 3 months of social content, press photos, video clips
Ceremony Night
📱

Live Social Coverage

Someone goes live on Instagram during the drumming performance and the threshold entry moment. Extends the ceremony audience from 100 people in the room to thousands watching in real time. Plan who does this in advance.

Assign: one person dedicated to live coverage only · Platform: Instagram Live
Extends ceremony reach to public audience in real time
Not Needed Before Opening

Printed brochures — nobody asks for printed brochures. Digital one-pagers do the same job at zero print cost.  ·  Merchandise — post-opening only, once there's a community to sell to.  ·  Video series — one brand video is enough pre-opening; a series requires real footage from inside an operating hub.  ·  Banners and event signage — only relevant for the physical space, not for outreach.  ·  Paid advertising creative — don't produce ad creatives until you have the photography and confirmed date to build them around.

Awareness & Lead Gen

All channels — what's active when

Every channel has a role and a timing. Nothing is rejected — everything is sequenced.

📱

WhatsApp / Personal Outreach

Highest-converting channel. Zero cost. Saran's existing network is the most powerful marketing asset in the plan. Schools, corporates, artists — all warm.

Active Now
📸

Organic Social (Instagram)

Existing event photography posted today. Fit-out "making of" series. Founder story. Cultural education content. Social media strategy and execution handled separately.

Active Now
🌐

Website

Coming-soon page live this week with email capture, schools PDF, and contact form. Full site ready July. Active lead capture forms for schools and corporates — not just a brochure.

Ship This Week
📍

Google Business Profile

New hub profile live with Al Quoz pin, renders, opening soon status. Collect reviews from past clients via WhatsApp now. 3 months of indexing starts today — not the week you open.

Set Up This Week
📰

PR — One Exclusive Story

One relationship. One journalist. One pre-opening exclusive feature. Time Out Dubai or Brownbook. Personal approach from Saran, not a press release. Press kit ready August 1.

August 1
🎙️

Ceremony as Content Engine

Not just an event — a content production day. Every moment is a 3-month supply of social content, press photography, and video assets. Dedicated capture team, shot list, 48-hour turnaround.

September
💼

LinkedIn (Organic)

Saran's founder voice — experiential learning, cultural education, community building. 3 posts per week. Reaches school decision-makers and corporate HR leaders at zero cost.

October onwards
🎯

Paid Social (Instagram/Facebook)

Activates mid-August at the earliest. Requires: confirmed date + real photography + live website. Boost performing organic posts only. Never boost for follower count.

Mid-August minimum
✉️

Email Marketing

CRM sequences activated from day one. Pre-opening: monthly updates. Post-opening: weekly. Primary relationship nurturing tool for schools and corporates who don't convert immediately.

Sequences from Oct
🔍

SEO & Journal Content

Cultural education articles, experiential learning, African heritage in Dubai. Takes 6 months to rank. Brief first 3 articles in October, see traffic results Q1 2027.

2027
▶️

YouTube

Long-form storytelling, documentary-style hub content, artist features. Needs real footage and an established audience. Start Q1 2027 once the community is built.

Launch Centrepiece · Tier 1

Opening ceremony

One evening. 80–120 guests. Every key person in one room. And a dedicated content production plan that turns the evening into 3 months of marketing assets.

Target: First Two Weeks of September 2026 · Al Quoz, Dubai · By Invitation Only
One Evening. The Founding Story.
Not a launch party — an institutional statement. Everything people believe about Afro Baobab for the next five years gets set in this evening. And every moment is captured for 3 months of content.
Embassies & Diplomats
20–25
Guinean Ambassador, UAE Ministry of Culture, African ambassadors
Press & Media
15–20
Editors, journalists, cultural writers, photographers
School Educators
15–20
Coordinators and trip planners, warmest contacts only
Corporate Leaders
10–15
HR Directors, L&D, CSR leads from active conversations
African Artists
10–15
Performers and visual artists — also a marketing channel
Founding Circle
10–15
Community leaders, diaspora advocates, strategic partners
Full Evening Agenda
18:00
Arrival & Welcome

Live kora music, African-inspired welcome drinks at the cultural café. Warm, unhurried arrival.

Live KoraWelcome Drinks
18:30
The Threshold Moment

Staggered guided entry — soundscape shift, first object reveal, curated lighting. The clip everyone films and shares.

Sensory EntryContent Moment #1
19:00
Founder Welcome Address — Saran

5–7 minutes. Why she built this, what she believes African culture deserves in Dubai. Personal, warm, vision-led.

Founder Story
19:10
Honorary Guest Remarks & Ribbon-Cutting

3–5 minutes. The institutional moment — the photo in every press piece, the image that signals legitimacy.

Ribbon-CuttingPress MomentContent Moment #2
19:20
Live Drumming Performance

15 minutes. Audience invited to participate. Drums at the centre, artists guide the room. The room comes alive.

Live PerformanceParticipationContent Moment #3
19:40
Guided Exhibition Walk

Small groups through all 6 zones. Artists stationed at each zone speaking to their work. Booking desk discreetly active.

6 ZonesArtist EncountersBooking Desk
20:15
Spoken Word & Poetry

10 minutes. African poet in the storytelling lounge. Intimate. The emotional centrepiece — the moment the room goes still.

Spoken WordContent Moment #4
20:30
Cultural Dinner & Live Music

African-inspired food, kora or Afrobeats acoustic set. Open networking. Every guest leaves with a confirmed next step.

African CuisineLive MusicNetworking
21:30
Close — Curated Take-Home

A cultural object, founding story card, or handcrafted piece from the hub. Something they keep and talk about.

Curated Gift
The Ceremony as a Marketing Channel
  • Dedicated photographer + videographer with a shot list for all 4 content moments
  • Someone live on Instagram during the drumming and threshold entry — extends reach to thousands watching in real time
  • 48-hour turnaround: curated photos and quotes sent to all press contacts before the weekend
  • One evening generates 3 months of social content, press photography, and video assets
  • Artists share the evening with their audiences — earned reach at zero cost
  • Every school and corporate guest leaves with a follow-up scheduled — booking desk active during dinner
01 · ENTRANCE
The Threshold Moment

Sensory transition at entry. The clip every guest films.

02 · PERFORMANCE
Live Drumming Circle

Participation. Rhythm connects the room.

03 · EMOTION
Spoken Word

The quiet centrepiece. The room goes still.

Contingency10% buffer7,000–11,000
ESTIMATED TOTALAED 73,000 – 117,500
Collaboration & Reach

UAE cultural ecosystem

The fastest path to credibility, school referrals, and corporate conversations runs through institutions that already have those relationships. The approach is always: give first, ask later.

Tier 1 — Start July
Dubai · Priority 1

Alserkal Avenue

60+ galleries and cultural spaces — your neighbours in Al Quoz. Established school programmes, corporate events, community audience, and mailing list. Walk into management this month. Express genuine respect, ask nothing yet.

→ July: introductory meeting with management team
Dubai · Priority 2

Dubai Culture & Arts Authority

Government body overseeing culture in Dubai. School referral network, cultural calendar, grant opportunities. Being listed as a partner opens institutional credibility.

→ July: introduction meeting, explore school network referral
Dubai · Priority 3

Jameel Arts Centre

One of the most respected contemporary art spaces in the region. School programmes, community events, international programming. A co-programming conversation could combine both communities.

→ Post-opening: co-programming proposal October/November
Tier 2 — After Opening
Abu Dhabi

Manarat Al Saadiyat

Community and cultural programming, school visits, family events — closest in format to Afro Baobab. Cross-referral for school groups is a natural conversation.

→ October: introductory visit, explore school referral exchange
Dubai

Dubai Design District (d3)

Creative community, designers, architects. Corporate and artist audience in one place. Good for visibility within the UAE's creative economy.

→ November: presence at community events
Dubai

DIFC Community

26,000 professionals in one square kilometre. DIFC has its own internal cultural events. A relationship with DIFC Arts opens access to an ideal corporate audience.

→ October: DIFC Arts introductory meeting
Sharjah

Warehouse421

Independent creative space, strong grassroots credibility with artists and the alternative cultural scene. Artist exchange and co-programming.

→ October: artist exchange conversation
Tier 3 — 2027
Sharjah · 2027

Sharjah Art Foundation

Most serious art institution in the UAE. International exhibitions, artist residencies, the Sharjah Biennial. Approach in 2027 with a year of programming behind you.

Abu Dhabi · 2027

Louvre Abu Dhabi

Global prestige, school programmes, international collaborators. A mention or collaboration here transforms institutional positioning. Too early now.

Dubai · 2027

Art Dubai & Design Week

Art Dubai is March, Design Week is November. Attend as visitors in 2026, exhibit or partner in 2027 once you have a track record as an institution.

Institutional Relationships

Embassies & connections

  • Guinean Ambassador — Warm · Priority 1Already in relationship. Prioritise as honorary guest. Warmest and most credible option for the ribbon-cutting moment. Start conversation this week.
  • UAE Ministry of CultureExisting ministerial contact — follow up this week. Institutional endorsement transforms positioning from private to public good.
  • African Embassies UAESenegal, Nigeria, South Africa, Kenya, Ethiopia. Pan-African credibility, diaspora reach, ceremony presence. Invite all to ceremony.
  • South African Business CouncilCorporate pipeline and institutional credibility. Post-opening: programme a joint event for Q4 2026.
  • African Artists Community UAEVisual artists, musicians, poets. Ceremony performers, exhibition artists, long-term collaborators and brand ambassadors.
Tier 2 · After Opening · October–December 2026

These are important — but only become powerful once the hub is open, the ceremony has happened, and there's something real to show. Doing these before opening is wasted energy. Build on what the ceremony created.

October

Corporate Outreach Campaign

Full HR/L&D/DEI outreach with ceremony content, testimonials, and bookable product. Now you have proof. AED 2–3k LinkedIn ads if pipeline warrants it.

October

LinkedIn Organic Cadence

Saran's founder voice — 3 posts per week. Experiential learning, cultural education, community reflections. Reaches school and corporate decision-makers at zero cost.

October

Referral Programme

After every happy school pilot: "Is there another coordinator I should speak to?" Built into the follow-up process. Multiplies reach at zero cost.

October

Institution Outreach

Dubai Culture, KHDA, Manarat Al Saadiyat, DIFC Arts. Walk in with opening coverage, photos, school numbers. Much stronger position than approaching before opening.

October–Nov

Case Study Production

Document 2–3 school visits and 2 corporate experiences. Photo + video + testimonial. Becomes primary sales asset for 2027 school calendar outreach.

November

PR Second Wave

"First 30 days" impact story. Concrete numbers: schools welcomed, teams hosted, community built. Pitch to same media contacts from the ceremony.

November

Business Council Events

British Business Group, South African Business Council, French Business Council. Speaking slots or event partnerships. Reaches corporate HR and L&D audience directly.

Dec

Holiday Programming

Cultural experiences for the Dubai winter season. Tourist audience arrives. Family programming, gifting experiences. First seasonal revenue spike.

Tier 3 · 2027 and Beyond

All major institutional partnerships — Sharjah Art Foundation, Louvre Abu Dhabi, Art Dubai, GESS Dubai conference, YouTube channel, resident artist programme, and international African art networks — are 2027 targets. They require a track record that September 2026 will begin building. The ceremony, the school pilots, and the first 90 days of programming create the foundation these relationships need to be credible. Not now. Then.

Risk Register

Key risks

!
Honorary guest not confirmed in time
Longest lead-time item. Three candidates in parallel outreach this week. No ceremony without this.
!
CRM not live before outreach generates leads
Every untracked school reply is a lost booking. Must be set up before any further outreach.
!
School calendar window closing
Dubai schools lock annual trip calendars in June/July. Every week of delay costs a full term of revenue.
!
Opening with empty calendar
10 confirmed school pilots + 5 corporate pilots is the minimum threshold before opening.
~
Fit-out delay pushes opening past September
All outreach continues. Rebook confirmed schools for October. Ceremony shifts, pipeline doesn't stop.
~
Photography dependency delays ads and press kit
Book photographer now. Shoot at 70% fit-out completion — don't wait for full.
This Week — 8 Non-Negotiable Actions
Sequenced by impact. The ones at the top cost the most if delayed even one more week.

Warm school calls start today — 20 existing contacts, reservation framing, PDF + testimonials attached

Honorary guest outreach today — 2–3 candidates in parallel, 6–8 week lead time, no ceremony without this

CRM setup — Zoho CRM, 5 segments, welcome sequences, live before next outreach wave

Post existing event photos today — proven content, drumming, workshops, diplomatic events, no new assets needed

Website ships to live domain — stop iterating, a live page beats a perfect unpublished one

Google Business Profile live — Al Quoz pin, renders, "Opening Soon", then WhatsApp past clients for reviews

School pricing agreed internally — blocking all outreach materials from being finalised

Ceremony date confirmed + vendor quotes — fit-out team alignment + 2–3 quotes for catering, AV, styling

Afro Baobab Cultural Hub
Complete Go-to-Market Plan · Phased & Prioritised · July 2026 · Confidential · Al Quoz, Dubai
